payload.answer: mem_new was returned because it is the newer current memory for Sam from Goldman. mem_old was filtered because it is superseded and therefore not valid as the present recall target.
payload.decision: Recall returned mem_new because it is the current, unsuperseded memory about Sam from Goldman; mem_old was excluded because it is marked or inferred as superseded by a newer memory.

entity_resolution.reason: The recall result favors the newest non-superseded memory for the same subject/source pairing, while the older competing memory is filtered out as superseded.

payload.intent: Explain why recall selected mem_new for Sam from Goldman and filtered mem_old as superseded.

memory_cards.statement: mem_new is the active memory for Sam from Goldman and should be used as the current recall target.
mem_old refers to an earlier memory about Sam from Goldman that has been superseded and should not be treated as current.

details: Current memory retained; superseded memory filtered.

repair_options: If mem_old should remain accessible, remove or relax its superseded flag.
If mem_new is incorrect, update the supersession relationship so the intended memory is current.
